channel.title: NYT > Style
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce Are Married
item.description: The couple shared the news through a digital advertisement outside Madison Square Garden, as well as a news release from Swift’s longtime publicist.
item.dc:creator: Madison Malone Kircher
item.media:credit: Jutharat Pinyodoonyachet for The New York Times
item.media:description: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce announced their nuptials on a digital advertisement outside Madison Square Garden.

item.title: Taylor Swift Wears Dior Dress by Jonathan Anderson for MSG Wedding
item.description: The pop star said “I do” to a gown designed by Jonathan Anderson, the French house’s creative director.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Friedman
item.media:credit: Federico Sorrentino for The New York Times
item.media:description: Jonathan Anderson, who joined Dior just last year, has been charged with revitalizing its global profile.

item.title: With Its Celebrity Guests, Taylor Swift’s Wedding Rivals the Met Gala
item.description: Top-secret celebrity guest lists! No cell phones! Big fashion moments! S.U.V.s galore! New York usually gets only one party like this a year.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Friedman
item.media:credit: TheStewartofNY/GC Images, via Getty Images; Brendan Mcdermid/Reuters; Backgrid

item.title: Travis Kelce Twins With Taylor in Dior
item.description: Both in couture looks by Jonathan Anderson, the couple showed they were on the same page.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Friedman and Joseph Bernstein
item.media:description: Travis Kelce doesn’t mind taking fashion risks, but for his wedding he went with one of the most storied French fashion houses.

item.title: Taylor Swift and Donald Trump Are Both Celebrating July 4th Weekend in Their Own Way.
item.description: Fourth of July weekend is shaping up to be a cultural moment of contrasting and comparing two of the most famous Americans, Taylor Swift and Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Jesse McKinley and Madison Malone Kircher
item.media:credit: Haiyun Jiang/The New York Times; Cassidy Araiza for The New York Times

item.title: Chanel Is Buying Charvet, the World’s Oldest Shirt Maker
item.description: The French luxury group is acquiring Charvet, a Parisian brand that dates to the 1830s, in a bid to add some men’s wear savoir-faire to its portfolio.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Friedman
item.media:description: Jacob Elordi wearing a Chanel x Charvet shirt for a January talk show appearance. According to Chanel’s president of fashion, Charvet’s brother-and-sister owners began mulling a sale after the success of that collaboration.

item.title: The Divorce Crusader
item.description: Part activist, part influencer, Adina Sash will do anything to rescue Orthodox women she sees as trapped in their marriages.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Bernstein
item.media:credit: Ahmed Gaber for The New York Times
item.media:description: Under the handle Flatbush Girl, Adina Sash deploys an internet-savvy playbook to name and shame men who won’t grant their wives Jewish divorce.

item.title: How to Dress for a Heat Wave
item.description: Advice for choosing the best fabrics and styles to help cool you down in extreme heat.
item.dc:creator: Yola Mzizi
item.media:description: Wearing natural fibers, loose-flowing silhouettes and light colors can help keep you cool.

item.title: When Did Bare Nails Become a Status Symbol?
item.description: From a “Love Story” plotline to runways and street wear, minimal or nude nails are everywhere.
item.dc:creator: Isha Sharma
item.media:description: Zoë Kravitz has sometimes donned the “invisible French manicure.”

item.title: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce Unite in Marriage: An Imagined New York Times Wedding Announcement
item.description: This traditional wedding announcement marks the union of the pop star and football player, who exchanged vows on Friday at Madison Square Garden.
item.dc:creator: Rosalie R. Radomsky
item.media:description: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce held a wedding ceremony at Madison Square Garden in Manhattan on July 3.

item.title: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce’s American Royal Wedding
item.description: A mega-famous popstar and a Super Bowl champion celebrated their love in Midtown Manhattan, announcing both their union and their cultural domination.
item.dc:creator: Amanda Hess
item.media:description: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce celebrated their marriage, and the culmination of a very public courtship, with a star-studded event at Madison Square Garden.

item.title: To Beat the Heat, the Ultrarich Have Snow Rooms
item.description: Indoors, for a fortunate few, it’s always a winter wonderland.
item.dc:creator: Guy Trebay

item.title: What to Know About Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce’s Wedding Festivities
item.description: Yes, they got married. Yes, they held a huge event inside Madison Square Garden. No, the guests did not post much about it. (At least they haven’t yet.)
item.dc:creator: Matt Stevens, Jesse McKinley and Madison Malone Kircher
item.media:credit: Jutharat Pinyodoonyachet for The New York Times
item.media:description: A “JUST&T MARRIED!” sign on a video screen outside the wedding venue Friday evening.

item.title: A walk through Taylor Swift’s New York on her wedding day.
item.dc:creator: Gabriella Gershensen
item.media:description: The wrists of Matthew LaSpata, a tour guide, and one of his customers, adorned with Taylor Swift-themed bracelets.

item.title: Scenes From Taylor Swift’s NYC Wedding: Fans, Stars and More
item.description: The scene outside Taylor Swift’s wedding in Manhattan had the drama and emotion of one of her albums.
item.dc:creator: Madison Malone Kircher and Emma G. Fitzsimmons
item.media:credit: Lanna Apisukh for The New York Times

item.title: How marriage appears in Taylor Swift’s lyrics.
item.dc:creator: Victor Mather

item.title: Photos: See Outside Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce’s Wedding at MSG
item.description: The couple’s private celebration created a public spectacle outside Madison Square Garden.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.media:credit: Jutharat Pinyodoonyachet for The New York Times

item.title: Fans gather outside Taylor Swift’s former and current New York homes.
item.description: Some fans braved the heat to snap photos outside the home she owns in TriBeCa as well as nearby Cornelia Street in the West Village.
item.dc:creator: Nia Decaille
item.media:credit: Natalie Keyssar for The New York Times
item.media:description: Fans take photos outside Taylor Swift’s former apartment at 23 Cornelia Street in Manhattan on Friday.

item.title: Why Did Adam Sandler Officiate Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce’s Wedding?
item.description: The actor played a special — and surprising to some — role in the Swift-Kelce nuptials.
item.dc:creator: Jesse McKinley
item.media:credit: Matt Winkelmeyer/Getty Images for Palm Springs International Film Society
item.media:description: Adam Sandler at the Palm Springs International Film Awards in January. A representative for Taylor Swift confirmed that the actor officiated the pop star’s nuptials.

item.title: Private Jets Work Overtime as Swift Wedding, World Cup and America250 Converge on New York
item.description: Private aviation companies are facing one of their busiest weekends of the year as thousands of affluent visitors descend on the city.
item.dc:creator: Christine Chung
item.media:description: Airplanes sit parked on the tarmac at New Jersey’s Teterboro Airport.

item.title: A timeline of Taylor Swift’s love story.
item.description: From Joe Jonas to Jake Gyllenhaal and Joe Alwyn, the singer dated several actors and musicians over the years, some of whom inspired her music.
item.dc:creator: Madison Malone Kircher

item.title: Do we know anything about Taylor and Travis’s wedding party?
item.description: A few clues that point to some bridesmaid and groomsman candidates.
item.dc:creator: Alisha Haridasani Gupta
item.media:description: Alana Haim, Taylor Swift and Este Haim attended Game 4 of the 2026 NBA Finals. The Haim sisters, longtime friends and collaborators of Swift, have fueled speculation about who might ultimately make up Swift’s wedding party.

item.title: Might an ice sculpture from a local artisan grace the Garden?
item.dc:creator: Matt Stevens
item.media:description: Okamoto ice sculptures were being delivered across the city.

item.title: What Time Is Taylor Swift Getting Married? See the Wedding Schedule at MSG.
item.description: An internal memo for the New York Police Department laid out a schedule of events for a 1,000-person celebration on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Emma G. Fitzsimmons
item.media:credit: Jutharat Pinyodoonyachet for The New York Times
item.media:description: Black SUVs arrive near Madison Square Garden as part of a well-orchestrated transportation plan for Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce’s celebration.

item.title: Swift fans brave sweltering heat.
item.dc:creator: Judson Jones
item.media:credit: Natalie Keyssar for The New York Times

item.title: What’s the wedding dress code?
item.dc:creator: Madison Malone Kircher and Chelsia Rose Marcius
item.media:description: Travis Kelce’s white-tie ensemble here would be a touch too formal for a black-tie event.

item.title: Prince William Goes on Travis Kelce’s ‘New Heights’ Podcast Before His Wedding
item.description: The British royal didn’t offer marriage advice. Instead, he discussed football, soccer and reminisced about when they all met in London during the Eras tour.
item.dc:creator: Ishani Desai

item.title: Stevie Nicks Is Expected to Perform With Taylor Swift at MSG Wedding Celebration
item.description: The bride did a sound check with the music legend, according to one person with knowledge of the event. A second person, who appeared to be working the event, was overheard describing a performance by Paul McCartney.
item.dc:creator: Madison Malone Kircher
item.media:description: Stevie Nicks is expected to perform with Taylor Swift on her wedding day at Madison Square Garden.

item.title: A Garden wedding: seating for about 20,000.
item.description: Madison Square Garden has already been the venue of high-profile weddings — real, fictitious and mass celebrations.
item.dc:creator: Rosalie R. Radomsky
item.media:description: The musician Sly Stone and the model-actress Kathy Silva smile after exchanging vows at Madison Square Garden on June 5, 1974, turning the arena into an unforgettable wedding venue.

item.title: Fans Gather Outside the Wedding of the Year at Madison Square Garden
item.description: As temperatures climbed to nearly 100 degrees and police cordoned off streets around the arena, devoted Swift fans from across the country gathered in anticipation of Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce’s celebration.
item.dc:creator: Emma G. Fitzsimmons and Madison Malone Kircher
item.media:description: People gathered outside Madison Square Garden in Manhattan, despite sweltering heat and increased security in the area.

item.title: Here’s what to know about the festivities.
item.dc:creator: Jesse McKinley and Madison Malone Kircher

item.title: Let’s revisit their engagement.
item.dc:creator: Madison Malone Kircher

item.title: Trump Nods at Taylor Swift in Posts on Social Media
item.description: In a series of posts, the president addressed Taylor Swift on the weekend of her wedding festivities, using her imagery and words.
item.dc:creator: Jesse McKinley

item.title: Good News for Bill Cunningham Fans
item.description: The New York Historical announced a new show, “We All Dress for Bill” with selections from parties, fashion shows and the street, coming in 2027.
item.dc:creator: Kellina Moore

item.title: How Celebrity Wedding Planners Would Decorate MSG for Taylor Swift
item.description: Dua Lipa’s planner envisioned olive trees and thousands of string lights. LeBron James’s planner took inspiration from the Eras Tour.
item.dc:creator: Madison Malone Kircher
item.media:credit: Adrienne Grunwald for The New York Times
item.media:description: A poster outside the Garden was the closest most spectators came to catching a glimpse of the bride at her venue this week.

item.title: Karlie Kloss, Jason Sudeikis and More Guests Arrive at Taylor Swift’s Wedding
item.description: The list includes Reese Witherspoon, the Haim sisters, and Karlie Kloss and Joshua Kushner, among many other celebrities, athletes and musicians.
item.dc:creator: Sadiba Hasan
item.media:description: Selena Gomez, a close friend of Taylor Swift, shared an Instagram Stories video in formal wear from New York on Thursday, seemingly en route to Swift and Travis Kelce’s rehearsal dinner, as other celebrity friends of the couple arrived in the city.

item.title: The Most Lavish Celebrity Weddings, From the Royals to Taylor Swift
item.description: We hear the couple has plans this weekend in New York City, as they look for their ever after.
item.dc:creator: Victor Mather
item.media:description: Posh and Becks, engaged.

item.title: What Does It Cost to Take Over Madison Square Garden?
item.description: It’s not just rent. There’s security, insurance, permits and other costs, not to mention extra police presence.
item.dc:creator: Alisha Haridasani Gupta, Ishani Desai, Maria Cramer and Maia Coleman
item.media:credit: Jutharat Pinyodoonyachet for The New York Times
item.media:description: Preparations at Madison Square Garden on Thursday.

item.title: What We Found After He Was Gone
item.description: Jeff was Billy’s friend and my lover. Was he also Cupid?
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Laura Nelson

item.title: A Relationship That Forced Two Magicians to Tip Their Hand
item.description: For Jamie Allan and Natalie Villalonga-Stanton, the daring feats they performed reinforced their mutual reliance and trust.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Goodman

item.title: On Military Leave Long Enough to Get Married
item.description: After graduating from the U.S. Naval Academy, Emma Shattuck and Anders Gulbrandson dated face-to-face about 22 percent of the time.
item.dc:creator: Rosalie R. Radomsky

item.title: He Wrote Hollywood Love Stories. It Took 12 Years to Finish His Own.
item.description: Michael Elliot, a screenwriter whose rom-com hits include “Brown Sugar” and “Just Wright,” married Adia May, a lawyer, more than a decade after they first met at a Los Angeles airport.
item.dc:creator: Charreah K. Jackson

item.title: By the Fourth Date, They Created a Shared Google Calendar
item.description: After several dead-end dates, Barry Pump had resigned himself to remaining a bachelor, but then he met Shreyans Goenka and bonded over their shared penchant for meticulous planning.
item.dc:creator: Shivani Vora

item.title: On Eve of Taylor Swift’s Wedding, Rehearsal Dinner Kicks Off Weekend of Events
item.description: The event for about 100 guests at Madison Square Garden kicked off a weekend of celebration for the pop star and the athlete, as celebrities arrived in town and reinforcements were delivered.
item.dc:creator: Madison Malone Kircher
item.media:credit: Jutharat Pinyodoonyachet for The New York Times
item.media:description: Tent structures were put in place, security was beefed up, and streets were closed as the couple prepared for their mega celebrations.

item.title: The Pants Debate at Men’s Fashion Weeks
item.description: Pants can be a source of anxiety in male wardrobes. Our men’s fashion reporter, Jacob Gallagher, unpacks the ongoing debate from Men’s Fashion Weeks in Paris and Milan.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Gallagher, Haimy Assefa and Laura Salaberry

item.title: How New Yorkers Dressed for the Heat Wave
item.description: The streets became a backdrop for crop tops, World Cup gear and at least one aloha shirt.
item.dc:creator: Yola Mzizi and Hiroko Masuike
item.media:description: One way to beat the heat? Skateboarding down a shady sidewalk.

item.title: The Logistics Behind Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce’s Madison Square Garden Wedding
item.description: The pop star is poised to host a wedding celebration at Madison Square Garden. The logistics in Midtown Manhattan over the July 4 weekend are complex.
item.dc:creator: Emma G. Fitzsimmons
item.media:credit: Dave Sanders for The New York Times
item.media:description: Forklifts unloaded large, plastic-wrapped cargo outside Madison Square Garden in Manhattan on Wednesday, a glimpse of the extensive logistics behind Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce’s wedding celebration.

item.title: Tiny Love Stories: ‘I Don’t Want an Old Man’
item.description: Modern Love in miniature, featuring reader-submitted stories of no more than 100 words.

item.title: Is Women’s Japanese Wrestling Fashion’s Favorite New Sport?
item.description: Olympia Le-Tan’s fashion career has taken her from Chanel to the world of Sukeban.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Iredale and Gabby Jones
item.media:description: The championship fight in May was Sukeban’s most ambitious yet. Here, Bingo, right, and Delirious Dolly compete in runway-ready custom fits.

item.title: The Dress Code for Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce’s Wedding? Expect a Black-Tie Event.
item.description: New details have emerged about the celebration at Madison Square Garden, widely believed to be a wedding, including a dress code.
item.dc:creator: Madison Malone Kircher and Chelsia Rose Marcius
item.media:description: Travis Kelce’s white-tie ensemble here would be a touch too formal for a black-tie event.

item.title: John Loring, Longtime Design Director at Tiffany & Company, Dies at 86
item.description: One of the country’s influential tastemakers, he oversaw the rapid expansion of Tiffany in the midst of seismic shifts in luxury retail.
item.dc:creator: Clay Risen
item.media:credit: Steve Campbell/Houston Chronicle, via Getty Images
item.media:description: John Loring in 2008. In 1979, he became the design director at Tiffany, a position he held for the next 30 years.

item.title: Men’s Style Gets Colorful and Strange
item.description: As Paris Men’s Fashion week winds to a close, see what our critic thought about shows at Celine, Soshiotsuki, Willy Chavarria and Comme des Garçons Homme.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Gallagher
item.media:credit: Clockwise from top left, Tom Nicholson/Reuters; Soshiotsuki; Laurent Hou/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images; Celine

item.title: Marc Jacobs Looks on the Bright Side
item.description: His brand has a new owner and the future may be uncertain, but color him ready.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Friedman

item.title: Street Style Look of the Week: A Sheer Top That ‘Doesn’t Make Sense’
item.description: A jewelry designer with experience in men’s wear looks for the “something that doesn’t make sense” that elevates an outfit.
item.dc:creator: Simbarashe Cha

item.title: How Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce Could Reveal Wedding Details
item.description: The playbook for celebrities’ wedding reveals has become a careful choreography — especially when you’re one of the biggest pop stars in the world.
item.dc:creator: Madison Malone Kircher
item.media:description: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce in Brooklyn last month. Since the couple announced their engagement last year, they have only hinted at a few details of their nuptials.

item.title: Naomi Osaka Serves Up a Fashion Slam at Wimbledon
item.description: The tennis player once again offered some on-court couture.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Friedman
item.media:description: Naomi Osaka, the 14th-ranked women’s tennis star, took to the grass for her first Wimbledon match of the year wearing an all-white ensemble by the Japanese designer Hana Yagi.
